title: indigofera suffruticosa
intro: Indigofera suffruticosa, also known as small-leaf indigo, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activity. The health benefits of Indigofera suffruticosa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
